mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously without requiring additional triggers, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 8% signals labor market slack and economic weakness, reducing corporate earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism reflects market repricing of growth and profitability in slack labor conditions.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when UNRATE > 8.0% are not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 700k units signals weak residential demand and reduced forward economic momentum. This demand weakness propagates to equity valuations over a 60-day horizon as market participants revise growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 698k units near trigger threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when HOUST < 700k are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and 60d returns exceeds -0.10 in absolute value in fewer than 3 of 5 rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and reduced productive capacity utilization. This supply weakness, when combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 228), creates stagflationary conditions that compress equity multiples and produce negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 229.098 indicates elevated price levels
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, mean 20-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect (INDPRO decline × high CPIAUCSL) explains less than 5% of variance in 20-day returns beyond single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 50k new jobs per month signals labor market momentum loss and forward economic deceleration. This deceleration triggers equity repricing over a 5-day horizon as high-frequency traders react to labor market weakness signals.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132821k indicates current employment level
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth < 50k are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or the signal triggers in fewer than 15% of months in the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both declining simultaneously indicate broad-based demand contraction across consumer and residential sectors. This dual demand weakness produces negative 60-day equity returns as the market reprices growth expectations downward across multiple demand channels.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND HOUST < 700k units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 407805M and HOUST at 698k indicate demand pressure
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across consumer and housing
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, mean 60-day returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal explains less than 8% of variance in 60-day returns beyond single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 230 combined with federal funds rate remaining below 0.5% signals monetary policy lag—real rates deeply negative. This negative real rate regime produces positive equity returns over 20 days as investors flee cash and seek real asset protection.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 229.098 and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% indicate negative real rates
regime_hint: negative real rate environment with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, mean 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect explains less than 6% of variance in 20-day returns beyond single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth trajectory below 2% annualized signals weak economic expansion that constrains corporate earnings growth. In this low-growth regime, equity valuations compress and produce negative 60-day forward returns as investors downgrade growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13429.0B indicates current real output level
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of sub-2% GDP growth are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
